setting.note: It opens on the gun Zam-Zammah outside the Lahore Museum, both still there, then walks the Grand Trunk Road the length of northern India. The anchor city is Lahore, now in Pakistan; the road and the Great Game carry the book east to Benares and north into the Himalayan foothills, so the bounding box spans the whole run from the Punjab to the Ganges.

rights.originalWork: Public domain. Rudyard Kipling, first published 1901; the author died in 1936.
rights.territoryNotes: Public domain in the United States, and in the United Kingdom and the European Union, where the author died more than 70 years ago (1936).
rights.attribution: Rudyard Kipling, Kim (1901). Text from Project Gutenberg.

palette.note: The green-arched length of the Grand Trunk Road, the river of life that carries the whole book, taken straight from the text. Clears WCAG AA on paper.

kim.name: Kim (Kimball O'Hara)
kim.colorSourceQuote: Red Bull on a green field
